name: Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale (RASS)
description: 10-level clinician-rated scale for assessing level of consciousness, sedation and agitation in adult ICU patients

en.name: Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale (RASS)
en.shortDescription: 10-level clinician-rated scale for assessing level of consciousness, sedation and agitation in adult ICU patients
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context The RASS (Richmond Agitation–Sedation Scale) is a 10-level clinician-rated scale for assessing the level of consciousness, sedation, and agitation in adult intensive care unit patients. It can be administered in 30–60 seconds and does not require special training [1]. The scale uses three sequential assessment steps — observation, response to auditory stimulation, and response to physical stimulation — to assign a single score: - +4 combative - +3 very agitated - +2 agitated - +1 restless - 0 alert and calm - −1 drowsy - −2 light sedation - −3 moderate sedation - −4 deep sedation - −5 unarousable Positive scores denote agitation, zero denotes a calm and alert state, and negative scores denote sedation. ## Included Concepts This concept set contains the OMOP-standard SNOMED concept for the scale: - `1447532` — Richmond Agitation Sedation Scale (`1345048008`, Staging / Scales, Standard) The non-standard SNOMED Observable Entity `1450955` (Richmond Agitation Sedation Scale score, `1345050000`) maps to this standard concept (OMOP `Maps to`), so recorded score values stored against the source code are captured through standard-concept resolution. ## References [1] Sessler CN, Gosnell MS, Grap MJ, et al. The Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale: validity and reliability in adult intensive care unit patients. *Am J Respir Crit Care Med*. 2002;166(10):1338-1344. doi:10.1164/rccm.2107138. PMID: 12421743

fr.name: Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale (RASS)
fr.shortDescription: Échelle à 10 niveaux cotée par le clinicien pour évaluer le niveau de conscience, de sédation et d'agitation chez les patients adultes de réanimation
fr.longDescription: ## Définition et contexte clinique Le RASS (Richmond Agitation–Sedation Scale) est une échelle à 10 niveaux cotée par le clinicien pour évaluer le niveau de conscience, de sédation et d'agitation chez les patients adultes de réanimation. Son administration prend 30 à 60 secondes et ne nécessite pas de formation spécifique [1]. L'échelle utilise trois étapes séquentielles d'évaluation — observation, réponse à la stimulation auditive et réponse à la stimulation physique — pour attribuer un score unique : - +4 combatif - +3 très agité - +2 agité - +1 nerveux - 0 éveillé et calme - −1 somnolent - −2 sédation légère - −3 sédation modérée - −4 sédation profonde - −5 non éveillable Les scores positifs indiquent une agitation, zéro correspond à un état calme et éveillé, et les scores négatifs indiquent une sédation. ## Concepts inclus Ce concept set contient le concept SNOMED standard OMOP de l'échelle : - `1447532` — Richmond Agitation Sedation Scale (`1345048008`, Staging / Scales, Standard) Le concept SNOMED Observable Entity non-standard `1450955` (Richmond Agitation Sedation Scale score, `1345050000`) se mappe vers ce concept standard (`Maps to` OMOP) ; les valeurs de score enregistrées sous le code source sont donc capturées via la résolution vers le concept standard. ## Références [1] Sessler CN, Gosnell MS, Grap MJ, et al. The Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale: validity and reliability in adult intensive care unit patients. *Am J Respir Crit Care Med*. 2002;166(10):1338-1344. doi:10.1164/rccm.2107138. PMID: 12421743

createdByDetails.affiliation: Univ Rennes, CHU Rennes, INSERM, LTSI-UMR 1099, Rennes, France
createdByDetails.profession: Medical Informatics and Intensive Care Physician

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Replaced SNOMED Staging/Scales concept 36684829 (Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale, US Edition, Standard) with SNOMED Observable Entity 1450955 (Richmond Agitation Sedation Scale score, code 1345050000, Non-standard). Rationale: the Observable Entity represents the recorded RASS score value rather than the assessment tool, which better matches the intended use of this concept set.

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Switched to the standard SNOMED concept for the scale. A standard concept (the scale itself) is preferred over a more specific but non-standard 'score' concept, which should not be used directly in OMOP. The standard target is the international SNOMED concept, not the US-Edition one referenced in v1.1.1.
